nodes.label: AI as creative copywriting assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI producing generic word salad without refinement
nodes.grounding_span: I rely heavily on AI tools for content creation

nodes.label: AI as marketing content generator
nodes.label_negative: AI causing cliche or wrong tone
nodes.grounding_span: It makes me feel a bit more confident in the marketing copy before I hand it over to a client but there are also issues like sounding too cliche or not finding the right tone

nodes.label: AI as productivity multiplier for small teams
nodes.label_negative: AI as job threat for large team employees
nodes.grounding_span: It helps me do more work in a short amount of time and offer a higher level of service

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: I then ask for two or three versions depending on how I feel about it

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI model inconsistency across days or projects
nodes.grounding_span: a model will be great one day and not as great the next week or for a different project

nodes.label: AI context limits and subscription barriers
nodes.label_negative: Unlimited context and unified AI environment
nodes.grounding_span: The context limits are something I bump into quite often and arbitrary limits put in place by AI companies to limit usage is really annoying

nodes.label: Quality of creative output
nodes.grounding_span: being able to feel confident that what I have is going to work

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: It helps me do more work in a short amount of time

nodes.label: Client success and satisfaction
nodes.grounding_span: delivering something closer to the high end marketing firms as an individual working from home

nodes.label: Creative autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I'm driving the decisions but I get AI to identify weaknesses

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for copywriting and ideation
nodes.grounding_span: I rely heavily on AI tools for content creation

nodes.label: Frustration with AI inaccuracies in data copying
nodes.grounding_span: It makes me feel a bit more confident in the marketing copy

nodes.label: Frustration with AI when it deviates from tone
nodes.grounding_span: issues like sounding too cliche or not finding the right tone

nodes.label: Optimism about AI empowering solo founders
nodes.grounding_span: I'm highly optimistic. I think the programming side of things and AI are more concerning than marketing and as a solo founder I'm not as worried about being put out of work

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors
nodes.grounding_span: a model will be great one day and not as great the next week or for a different project

nodes.label: Frustration with context limits and subscription barriers
nodes.grounding_span: The context limits are something I bump into quite often and arbitrary limits put in place by AI companies to limit usage is really annoying

nodes.label: Iterative refinement through conversation
nodes.grounding_span: I start a new conversation or choose a new model and send it through and ask for critical feedback

nodes.label: Strategic experimentation and A/B testing
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes I'll do an A/B test and show both to the client and we sorta pick out what is best or send both out and display copy A to one visitor and copy B to another visitor
